matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: KCNH2 long-QT model (KCNH2 hERG channelopathy model)
KCNH2 drug-sensitivity model (hERG pharmacology and QT-risk model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNH2 hERG potassium-channel review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology and Long-QT syndrome review context, Drug-induced QT prolongation biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed KCNH2 long-QT model dosage suggests this sample carries the KCNH2 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNH2 hERG potassium-channel review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology and Long-QT syndrome review context, Drug-induced QT prolongation biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as KCNH2 hERG channelopathy model. KCNH2 long-QT model is curated as a KCNH2 marker for Long-QT syndrome review context, Drug-induced QT prolongation biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched KCNH2 long-QT model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the KCNH2 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNH2 hERG potassium-channel review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology and Long-QT syndrome review context, Drug-induced QT prolongation biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed KCNH2 variant places this sample in the curated KCNH2 research context. The local KCNH2 bundle is hERG potassium-channel review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. KCNH2 variants can alter or tag cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a KCNH2 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a KCNH2 hERG potassium-channel review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology and Long-QT syndrome review context, Drug-induced QT prolongation biology. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. KCNH2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a cardiac IKr current, ventricular repolarization, drug-induced QT sensitivity, trafficking, and arrhythmia biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local KCNH2 bundle is hERG potassium-channel review oriented.
